subject: Joint Distributions
question: The joint pdf of $X$ and $Y$ is given by $f_{XY}(x,y) = \begin{cases} x+y & 0 \le x, y < 1 \\ 0 & \text{Otherwise.} \end{cases}$ Are $X$ and $Y$ independent?
explanation: Let $I(x,y) = \begin{cases} 1 & 0 \le x < 1,\ 0 \le y < 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases}$ Then $f_{XY}(x,y) = (x+y)I(x,y)$ which clearly does not factor into a part depending only on $x$ and another depending only on $y$. Thus, by the previous theorem $X$ and $Y$ are dependent
